$description: Transit (yesid.dev brand) design tokens (DTCG). Adopted from yesid.dev for the STM citizen dashboard (slice-9.2). Dark-first four-colour infrastructure doctrine: ORANGE=interactive-only, YELLOW=wayfinding/hazard (never interactive), WHITE=reflective, BLACK=tape/structure. PLUS color.dataviz: a SEPARATE data-encoding scale (status/occupancy/severity/heatmap under dark+light; vehicle aliases under brand) kept OFF the semantic affordance tokens (--success/--destructive/--accent); solid (no alpha), theme-split, null=no-data never a sentinel. Generators in tools/tokens/build.ts produce tokens.css and the app.css @theme region. JavaScript motion tokens come from the immutable @yesid/motion customer snapshot. Run `bun run tokens:build`.

brand.$description: ROUND-4 DOCTRINE ROLES (amended round 5c). ORANGE (primary): interactive/signage — links, CTAs, focus, active states; the clickable hue for everything EXCEPT conversion. YELLOW (accent/accent-text/line-amber/accent-surface): wayfinding & hazard & highlight — overlines/section labels (.label-station), departure-board & status readouts, metric/number callouts, data-viz amber, selected states, hazard tape; plus ONE clickable job (round 5c): 'talk to Yesid' conversion buttons on the signage pair (≤1 per view) — never any other interaction. WHITE (reflective + dark foreground): reflective — key headline words on dark, catch-lights (edge-highlight), white-core métro dots/chips. BLACK (hazard-b/signage-bg + light border-strong): tape/structure — the #1C1814 signage family draws light-mode strong rules; dark mode's deep surfaces ARE the black voice.

primary.$description: ORANGE — interactive/signage role: links, buttons, focus rings, active states, the orange dot in 'yesid.' The ONLY clickable hue (doctrine). Light theme overrides to #A05500 for AA on cool-slate paper; dark re-pins #E07800 so .theme-dark scene pinning survives inside light pages.

accent.$description: YELLOW — wayfinding/hazard/highlight role AS SURFACE/ART only: hazard tape, lamps, selection, chip dots. NEVER body text — use accent-text. Round 5c: the ONLY clickable yellow is the conversion button ground (with signage-bg ink, accent-hover hover) — 'talk to Yesid' actions, ≤1 per view; everything else stays unclickable yellow. W5d cursor law: a terminal cursor always matches the color of the text it types (currentColor) — never a standalone accent.

primary-rgb.$description: RGB channels for rgb(var(--primary-rgb) / alpha) usage.

reflective.$description: WHITE — reflective role, theme-invariant: key headline words over guaranteed-dark grounds (photo gradients), white-core métro station dots, reflective chips on dark. Reflective material doesn't reskin. 16.13:1 on signage-bg; pair only with dark/photo-gradient grounds, never with paper surfaces.

hazard-a.$description: YELLOW role — hazard tape stripe A. Theme-invariant: real tape doesn't reskin.

hazard-b.$description: BLACK role — hazard tape stripe B, warm black, theme-invariant (light stays BLACK+YELLOW like real safety tape, never orange+white). 10.06:1 stripe pair.

signage-bg.$description: BLACK role — backlit station-sign chip background, theme-invariant (real signs don't reskin). The #1C1814 signage family also draws light-mode strong structural rules (light border-strong).

signage-text.$description: Station-sign digits on signage-bg. 10.06:1 in both modes.

signal-caution.$description: Signal-lamp alias — amber aspect (text form = accent-text).

map-stop-fill.$description: Map stop marker fill — amber on the dark board (dark default; dark inherits this :root value). Light theme overrides to a vivid reddish-orange so stops punch on the near-white map. Map-only token.

glow.$description: Brand orange GLOW (lamp/dot glows, e.g. the ToC counter dot). Theme-invariant, matching yesid.dev --glow; glows do not carry text so they stay vivid orange in both themes.

background.$description: Taste round 2: the amber/bakelite board is REJECTED — restored pre-GO2-W5 near-black. Deep neutral dark so orange reads as CONTRAST, never as a wash.

foreground.$description: Restored neutral white. WHITE doctrine role on dark: key headline words (hero line 1, section titles) ARE the reflective voice on the deep board. 16.84:1 bg · 15.91 card · 13.12 popover.

muted-foreground.$description: Restored neutral (GO-W2.2 value). Worst-case 4.73:1 on popover (6.07 bg, 5.74 card).

card.$description: Restored neutral panel one step above bg — still SOLID; alpha on --card is forbidden (grid must never bleed through).

terminal.$description: EQUALS --background by contract (taste round 2): terminals are the SITE background, solid/opaque — identity carried by chrome strip, rule border and mono type, never by surface color. contrast-floors.test.ts locks the equality.

border-strong.$description: Restored neutral emphasized rule (2.08:1 bg). Scale stays subtle < default < strong. Brand-loud structure now lives in --border-rule / --border-rule-accent. Round-4 BLACK doctrine: dark mode keeps deep neutral surfaces — the board itself is the black voice, so strong rules stay neutral here (light flips to the #1C1814 tape family).

primary.$description: Re-pin for .theme-dark subtrees inside light pages.

accent-text.$description: YELLOW role AS TEXT — wayfinding overlines (.label-station), departure-board/status readouts, metric/number callouts, station markers. Identical to --accent in dark; darkened amber ink in light. 10.50 bg · 9.92 card · 11.06 manifesto.

input.$description: GO2-W5: was #3A3A3A (1.8:1, latent 1.4.11 FAIL). Conforming dark form boundary — 3.31 bg / 3.12 card (recomputed on the restored neutrals); warm because form boundaries are interactive.

terminal-chrome.$description: Terminal titlebar/footer strip — neutral machined chrome one step BELOW the board (taste round 2: re-derived for the restored near-black bg).

terminal-ink.$description: Terminal output text (warm phosphor — type IS the terminal identity). 14.28:1 on terminal (= bg).

signal-lunar.$description: Lunar-white shunt aspect — neutral/inactive indicator dots. 11.59 card / 12.26 terminal.

lamp-bezel.$description: Signal-head target-plate ring behind lamps on busy surfaces (decorative).

line-amber.$description: Metro/track SVG stroke — the yellow line. 10.53:1 on bg.

accent-surface.$description: SOLID yellow-tint chip/selected-filter bg (no alpha — grid never bleeds). accent-text 8.24:1, primary 4.72:1, fg 13.22:1 on it.

grid-glow.$description: Sodium-lamp radial spill from top (dark only).

edge-highlight.$description: 1px inset top bevel on cards — panel catching light (decorative).

early.$description: Ahead of schedule. Glyph ▼. Steel-blue, distinct from on-time green.

empty.$description: Sequential violet ramp (NOT status hues). Low crowding.

full.$description: Crush — brightest, stands out on dark.

nodata.$description: NO DATA — never a sentinel value, never cell 0 (repeat_problem_score lesson).

tier-0.$description: Classed-tier ramp (S7 P4/P8): single-hue ORANGE luminance scale, calmest. Dark theme tier-0 is light amber (visible on the dark board); severity deepens to burnt-red. CVD-safe (luminance-ordered), brand-aligned, worst tier kept far from --primary.

background.$description: Cool-slate station paper — near-white blue-grey (yesid.dev light rebrand 2026-06-19).

foreground.$description: Cool near-black ink on slate paper.

muted-foreground.$description: GO2-W5 warm shift. Worst-case 4.75:1 on muted (5.14 bg/terminal, 5.64 card, 4.76 manifesto).

card.$description: SOLID enamel plate on the paper — cards lift above bg. Alpha forbidden.

terminal.$description: EQUALS --background by contract (taste round 2): the cream-console surface is rejected — terminals are the SITE background, solid/opaque; chrome/border/type carry the identity. contrast-floors.test.ts locks the equality.

border.$description: Taste round 2 HARDER: was #DCD3C2 (1.33:1, operator: too soft). 1.68:1 bg / 1.85 card — a drawn line, not a smudge.

border-subtle.$description: Hardened one step past the old default (1.47:1 bg).

border-strong.$description: Round 4 BLACK doctrine role — tape/structure: light-mode strong rules lean TRUE-DARK, joining the #1C1814 signage/hazard-b family (was #A08F70, 2.83:1). Black tape on paper: 15.82:1 bg / 17.36 card. Consumers: inline-code chip edges, MetroStation rail ties (black ties over the amber line).

success.$description: GO2-W5: was #15803D (4.16:1 on warmer muted). 5.33 bg / 5.85 card / 4.93 muted.

primary.$description: ORANGE interactive/signage on light — deepened to #A05500 for AA contrast on cool-slate paper.

accent-text.$description: Amber ink — YELLOW role AS TEXT (wayfinding overlines, departure-board/status readouts, metric/number callouts, station digits). Worst case 4.71:1 on terminal-chrome; 5.38 bg · 5.90 card · 4.98 muted · 4.98 manifesto.

input.$description: Form boundary (WCAG 1.4.11): 3.08 bg / 3.38 card; decorative --border stays lighter.

terminal-chrome.$description: Chrome strip (titlebar/footer) — a deeper paper step on the page-colored terminal body.

terminal-ink.$description: Console ink on the paper terminal. 10.69:1 on terminal (= bg).

signal-lunar.$description: Lunar shunt aspect on paper. 7.04 card / 5.93 muted.

lamp-bezel.$description: Target-plate ring so lamps sit on a plate in daylight (decorative).

line-amber.$description: The yellow line that survives paper — graphics ≥3:1 (3.14 bg / 3.44 card).

accent-surface.$description: SOLID yellow-tint chip bg. accent-text 5.19:1, primary 4.99:1, fg 15.27:1 on it.

grid-line-major.$description: Cool-slate grid: 8% foreground on near-white slate (cool, not warm-primary).

full.$description: Crush — darkest, stands out on paper.

nodata.$description: NO DATA — never a sentinel value, never cell 0.

tier-0.$description: Classed-tier ramp (S7 P4/P8): single-hue ORANGE scale for light theme, calmest = medium amber, deepening to burnt-red. CVD-safe (luminance-ordered), brand-aligned.

map-stop-fill.$description: Light-theme stop fill — vivid reddish-orange, brighter+redder than the #A05500 bus orange so stops read distinct on the near-white map.

border-brand.$description: Light-parity: stronger structural demarcation on paper (45%→60%).

shadow-glow-sm.$description: Light-parity soft shadow (replaces the dark board's orange glow).

shadow-glow-lg.$value: 0 0 24px color-mix(in srgb, var(--primary) 9%, transparent), 0 0 60px color-mix(in srgb, var(--primary) 4%, transparent)

mono.$value: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', 'JetBrains Mono', 'Fira Code', 'Cascadia Code', monospace

mobile.$description: Longform body copy inside article detail cards on mobile.

desktop.$description: Longform body copy inside article detail cards on desktop.

stat-value.$description: Large numeric value in compact article and listing summary cards.

listing-title.$description: Visible mobile title in blueprint listing headers.

control.$description: Button/control type size (matches yesid.dev; consolidated here).

tag.$description: Badge/tag/chip type size (matches yesid.dev; consolidated here).

tracking.$description: Letter-spacing scale (yesid.dev). tight/wide match yesid.dev; eyebrow = uppercase-overline tracking (matches the dominant .label-section 0.1em). Pure addition — nothing renders differently until a component consumes it. NOTE: the text.heading / text.micro SCALE re-align to the yesid scale is a deliberate follow-up, intentionally NOT done here.

eyebrow.$description: Uppercase overline/station-label tracking — matches the dominant .label-section eyebrow spacing (0.1em).

page-x.$description: Page gutter — matches the yesid brand base (clamp 1.5rem/4vw/5rem) as of design v0.3.0 (P5.3a·E4). The former 1rem mobile floor divergence was reconciled UP to the brand value; drift-register override retired.

glow-sm.$description: Glows ride --glow (theme-invariant added light) to match the yesid brand base as of design v0.3.0 (P5.3a·E4). Reconciled from the former --primary basis; drift-register override retired. --primary darkens in light mode for text contrast and would kill glows on paper, so glow color-mixes use --glow.

glow-lg.$value: 0 0 24px color-mix(in srgb, var(--glow) 15%, transparent), 0 0 60px color-mix(in srgb, var(--glow) 6%, transparent)

card.$value: 0 0 16px color-mix(in srgb, var(--primary) 8%, transparent), 0 2px 8px rgba(10, 7, 4, 0.35), inset 0 1px 0 var(--edge-highlight)
card.$description: GO2-W5: warm black term + platform-lamp top catch-light folded into the token — every shadow-card consumer gets the bevel for free.

sheet.$description: GO-W2.2: formalizes the TocPill bottom-sheet shadow.

surface.$description: GO-W2.2 luminance-ordered surface scale. Pure var() aliases — zero drift, theme-switching flows through. GO2-W5 flip: 1 now aliases var(--card) so every card-family consumer lifts one step above the page; use var(--background) directly for page surfaces. 0=terminal/code, 1=card (lifted), 2=canonical card, 3=side panels, 4=elevated chips, hero=manifesto/detail heroes.

border.$description: GO-W2.2 brand-border + hairline aliases (Constitution Section 13 card spec, defined ONCE) + the GO2-W5 taste-round-2 BOLD structural rules: lines, delimitations, dividers and section borders carry the two brand voices loudly in both modes while surfaces stay calm. Round 3 (operator): the whole structural grid is one step THICKER — rule/brand consumers draw at 2px (hazard tape sm 3px / md 6px, footer tape 3px), and light mode additionally raises --border-brand to 60% via the app.css hand region so paper structure reads slightly more pronounced than dark.

brand.$description: Card/panel edges — the brand grid. Taste round 2: raised 25% → 45%. Round 3: consumers draw it at 2px; light mode overrides to 60% (app.css hand region) — dark keeps 45%.

rule.$description: SOLID orange structural rule — terminal chassis, dashed filter delimitations, accent rails. Full-strength route-set voice (6.01:1 dark bg, 5.17:1 light bg). Round 3: drawn at 2px everywhere (chassis, .divider-dashed).

rule-accent.$description: SOLID yellow wayfinding rule — departure-board lines (footer status rule). #FFB627 dark (10.50:1), #B57F00 light (3.14:1). Never interactive. Round 3: drawn at 2px.
